eval-ci-hardening: Skill performed perfectly. sisakulint caught persist-credentials and timeout-minutes that baseline missed. This is the skill's strongest use case.
eval-supply-chain: Two issues: (1) Secrets were not reduced — SKILL.md has no guidance on secret-exposure remediation beyond what sisakulint auto-detects. (2) Final scan output was empty file — agent may have run sisakulint but failed to capture output, or the re-run step was skipped. SKILL.md step 4 says 're-run sisakulint and confirm zero findings' but doesn't emphasize saving the output.
eval-clean-workflow: Critical false positive: sisakulint flagged 13 missing-timeout-minutes on steps when job-level timeouts (15/10/20 min) were already set. The auto-fix then added redundant step-level timeouts. This is either a sisakulint bug (doesn't recognize job-level timeouts) or the SKILL.md should instruct the agent to skip step-level timeout findings when job-level is present. The without_skill baseline correctly identified the workflow as clean.
eval-ai-agent: sisakulint detected ai-action-prompt-injection but missed ai-action-unrestricted-trigger and ai-action-excessive-tools — both listed in rules.md but not fired. Either the rules are not yet implemented in sisakulint, or the test fixture doesn't trigger them. The without_skill baseline caught all three through general reasoning. SKILL.md should add manual remediation patterns for these AI rules.
